content: The Verge The Verge is about technology and how it makes us feel. Founded in 2011, we offer our audience everything from breaking news to reviews to award-winning features and investigations, on our site, in video, and in podcasts. 2026-07-22T13:36:40+00:00 https://www.theverge.com/rss/index.xml https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/01/verge-rss-large_80b47e.png?w=150&h=150&crop=1 David Imel https://www.theverge.com/?p=968200 2026-07-22T08:30:21-04:00 2026-07-22T09:00:00-04:00 While we wait for Apple's rumored foldable iPhone , Samsung is polishing a design it already has on the market. I just spent three hours with my hands on the new Z Fold 8 Ultra, and all I can think about is how Samsung finally made the crease invisible. Samsung says it achieved this thanks to its new Flex Titanium display tech. Its foldable screens now utilize a titanium film that sits below the OLED panel, which Samsung says has 20-times the mechanical stiffness as opposed to the polymer film it used in its old foldables. This film is bonded to a new titanium plate, and when used together, the crease that used to plague foldable devices is … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Dominic Preston https://www.theverge.com/?p=968663 2026-07-22T08:44:44-04:00 2026-07-22T09:00:00-04:00 The new Z Fold 8 is squat and wide, but don’t hold that against it. | Photo: David Imel / The Verge A year after overhauling its Z Fold phone with a radically thinner design, Samsung has changed things up again, this time altering the shape entirely. The Z Fold 8 is wider than any of Samsung's previous foldables, and shorter too, a size and shape somewhere between Samsung's traditional Flip and Fold models. In photos, I wasn't impressed by the squat shape. In person, to my surprise, it feels great, and almost immediately intuitive. If you want to get a feel for it yourself, grab your passport - the Z Fold 8 is about the same height and less than half an inch thinner. This isn't Samsung's only new foldable this year, and in fact, its line … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Victoria Song https://www.theverge.com/?p=968728 2026-07-22T07:28:53-04:00 2026-07-22T09:00:00-04:00 The squircle squad is here to stay. | Photo: David Imel / The Verge It's a year of refinement for the Galaxy Watch. With the new Galaxy Watch 9 and Galaxy Watch Ultra 2, which are being announced today, Samsung is more or less taking what people like about its watches and tweaking those elements to be better. The company has been doing this for the past few generations of its watch line, but this year's updates seem focused on shoring up Samsung's wearable weaknesses. Namely, battery life and preventive health features. The Ultra 2, now $50 more expensive at $699.99, gets a few key spec bumps. The 47mm watch packs in a whopping 800mAh battery - a major 35 percent increase from the 590mAh battery in the orig … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> David Imel https://www.theverge.com/?p=968883 2026-07-22T08:30:39-04:00 2026-07-22T09:00:00-04:00 Samsung's new Galaxy Z Flip 8 feels more like a regular phone than ever. It's thinner, lighter, and has a front screen you can do just about anything on. Whether that's a good or bad thing, I'm not quite sure yet. I spent three hours with Samsung's newest flip phone last week, and it doesn't look too different from last year's Z Flip 7 . It's still got a 6.9-inch FHD+ AMOLED display on the inside. It's still got a 4.1-inch Super AMOLED cover screen on the outside. It still starts at 12GB of RAM and 256GB of storage, and it still has a 4,300mAh battery. But this year Samsung has added even more features that make this thing a full-on phone w … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Stevie Bonifield https://www.theverge.com/?p=969208 2026-07-22T09:24:33-04:00 2026-07-22T09:00:00-04:00 Samsung's annual summer event kicked off on Wednesday morning with the company unveiling a new lineup of watches and foldables. Following weeks of rumors and leaks, Samsung announced the Galaxy Z Fold 8 with a completely new design that's shorter and wider than last year's Fold, a new "Ultra" Fold, an updated Flip model, and refreshed versions of the Galaxy smartwatches. If you weren't able to watch the event yourself, you can catch up with all of this year's biggest Galaxy Unpacked announcements below. The Galaxy Z Fold 8 launches with a new wide design The highlight of Galaxy Unpacked was the Galaxy Z Fold 8, which got a major redesign … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Emma Roth https://www.theverge.com/?post_type=vm_stream&p=967942 2026-07-22T09:36:40-04:00 2026-07-22T08:30:00-04:00 Samsung revealed its newest slate of foldables at this summer’s Galaxy Unpacked, which took place in London. In addition to a Galaxy Z Fold 8 that adopts a wider, passport-like form factor, Samsung showed off its new Galaxy Z Fold 8 Ultra with a crease you can barely see and a refreshed Z Flip 8 , which has some new features for its cover screen. We saw some new wearables as well, including the Galaxy Watch Ultra 2 that comes with a larger battery and a thinner case, alongside a Galaxy Watch 9 equipped with an upgraded chip. Samsung and Google revealed two new designs for their AI smart glasses launching later this year, too. You can follow along with all the news from Samsung’s July Galaxy Unpacked below. My FanCam is Samsung’s AI video editor for focusing on your favorites. Samsung’s newest foldable finally feels Ultra Samsung’s wider Z Fold 8 feels just right Samsung’s Galaxy Watch 9 and Ultra 2 bet big on battery I almost forgot Samsung’s Z Flip 8 was a foldable Samsung and Google’s smart glasses get two new designs. I’m at Galaxy Unpacked ready for Samsung’s new foldable. Samsung Galaxy Unpacked July 2026: How to watch Samsung can’t afford to play it safe with Apple’s first foldable looming ]]> Jess Weatherbed https://www.theverge.com/?p=968680 2026-07-22T05:29:02-04:00 2026-07-22T07:00:00-04:00 Content Seal sounds a lot like a less accessible and reliable version of SynthID. | Image: Meta / The Verge IIn March, Meta's Oversight Board called on the company to "meet its public commitments and employ its own tools" to help quell the spread of deceptive generative AI content across platforms. Meta responded in July by introducing Content Seal - an invisible watermarking technology that flags images generated by the company's new AI model. But it was basically a footnote buried in the company's announcement for its Muse image and video generation tools. As someone who spends a lot of time scrutinizing AI labeling systems, Content Seal doesn't fill me with confidence. There are already more established solutions, like C2PA Content Credentials … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Peter Nelson https://www.theverge.com/?p=968772 2026-07-21T21:15:28-04:00 2026-07-22T07:00:00-04:00 When it comes to enthusiast-geared Honda hardware, the Civic Si, Civic Type R, and Acura NSX often come to mind first for their revvy VTEC (Honda's form of variable valve timing) engines and playful chassis dynamics. The Prelude, on the other hand, less so. Instead, the Prelude is a technological study - still aimed at driving fun, but by way of integrating clever forms of engineering into a sleek grand touring coupe body. The second-generation Prelude's fuel injection was derived from F1. Then the third gen got four-wheel steering way back in the late '80s. The fourth gen? It was the first everyday Honda to inherit the NSX's VTEC on both t … Read the full story at The Verge. ]]> Jess Weatherbed https://www.theverge.com/?p=969137 2026-07-22T07:00:09-04:00 2026-07-22T06:12:13-04:00 The pledge was introduced in March, and has done little to quell concerns so far. | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images In the face of backlash to concerns the AI boom will increase consumer electricity bills, the largest utility companies and data center developers in the US are now promis
